title: Animal Migration Mechanisms

domain_resolution.context: Sensory and Navigation Ecology | Migration Biology | Animal Behavior and Cognition | Ecology

objective: Investigate the causal mechanisms behind animal migration, including visual landmarks, geographic cues, solar and stellar compass mechanisms, magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms, electromagnetic induction, and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Focus on cross-taxon and cross-environment mechanisms, treating evidence as context-compatible only when appropriate.
research_brief: Create a new natural-science research project and run the complete AutoGen closed-loop validation workflow: ZhiZhi literature retrieval and PaperGraph   expansion, TanXi primary-mechanism gap detection, Socrates evidence-bounded causal contracts and mediator discovery, MingLi competing falsifiable hypothesis generation with a   structured experimental protocol, YanZhen adversarial counterevidence retrieval and controversy matrix, DuZhi Socratic challenge, and BianLun evidence-weighted minimum-   commitment synthesis. Use semantic_scholar, arxiv, biorxiv, and pubmed where available; retrieve at least 50 results and import at least 20 eligible papers; run debate iteration   with up to 5 rounds. Topic: How do migratory animals know where they are and where they are going? Animal migration is regular seasonal movement among habitats and spans north-   south, east-west, ocean-land, altitudinal, and water-column movement. Treat this as a cross-taxon and cross-environment causal-mechanism question covering birds, insects,   mammals, fish, marine animals, and other organisms only when evidence is context-compatible. Compare visual landmarks and geographic cues; solar and stellar compass mechanisms;   magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms; electromagnetic induction in electrically sensitive aquatic animals; and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Do not   treat evidence from one species, sensory ecology, developmental stage, or migration environment as automatically transferable to another. For every candidate causal edge   preserve taxon, species, model system, migratory stage, environment, perturbation, readout, time scale, supporting sources, challenge sources, alternative pathways, applicable   boundary, and uncertainty state. Prioritize direct mechanism gaps, causal mediation gaps, contradictions, anomalies, and cross-hypothesis coupling over method-scenario-benchmark   coverage holes. YanZhen must actively search for counterevidence and ask whether magnetic mechanisms are necessary, sufficient, context dependent, or bypassed by visual,   celestial, olfactory, inertial, and learned-route cues. If a mediator remains unknown, return MECHANISM_DISCOVERY_REQUIRED with candidate mediators rather than inventing a   complete mechanism. Generate competing and falsifiable hypotheses; experimental protocols must remain execution-blocked unless domain experts explicitly supply model system,   intervention, controls, timepoints, primary and orthogonal readouts, replication, bias control, analysis plan, meaningful effect threshold, interval requirement, power   assumptions, rescue criteria, regime-shift tests, and reproducibility details. At the end, output project_id, PaperGraph paper count, primary scientific gaps, Socrates contract   states, generated hypothesis ids, YanZhen verdict, BianLun final decision, structured experiment protocol status, execution authorization status, remaining uncertainty, and the   next discriminating experiment.

papergraph.title: A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance
papergraph.citation: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1
papergraph.abstract: Various animals exhibit accurate navigation using environment cues. The Earth's magnetic field has been proved a reliable information source in long-distance fauna migration. Inspired by animal navigation, this work proposes a bionic and data-driven approach for long-distance underwater navigation. The proposed approach uses measured geomagnetic data for the navigation, and requires no GPS systems or geographical maps. Particularly, we construct and train a Temporal Attention-based Long Short-Term Memory (TA-LSTM) network to predict the heading angle during the navigation. To mitigate the impact of geomagnetic anomalies, we develop the mechanism to detect and quantify the anomalies based on Maximum Likelihood Estimation. We integrate the developed mechanism with the TA-LSTM, and calibrate the predicted heading angles to gain resistance against geomagnetic anomalies. Using the retrieved data from the WMM model, we conduct numerical simulations with diversified navigation conditions to test our approach. The simulation results demonstrate a resilience navigation against geomagnetic anomalies by our approach, along with precision and stability of the underwater navigation in single and multiple destination missions.

gap_signals.text: We integrate the developed mechanism with the TA-LSTM, and calibrate the predicted heading angles to gain resistance against geomagnetic anomalies.
gap_signals.supporting_reference: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1

gap_signals.text: A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance Various animals exhibit accurate navigation using environment cues.
gap_signals.supporting_reference: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1

gap_signals.supporting_reference: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1

gap_signals.text: The proposed approach uses measured geomagnetic data for the navigation, and requires no GPS systems or geographical maps.
gap_signals.supporting_reference: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1

domain_gate.reason: Preprint passed hard domain gates; citation count is not used as an exclusion criterion.

evidence.title: A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance
evidence.citation: Songnan Yang et al. (2024) A Bionic Data-driven Approach for Long-distance Underwater Navigation with Anomaly Resistance arXiv:2403.08808v1
evidence.scenario: various animals exhibit accurate navigation using environment
evidence.limitation: The simulation results demonstrate a resilience navigation against geomagnetic anomalies by our approach, along with precision and stability of the underwater navigation in single and multiple destination missions.

sub_hypotheses.falsification_condition: Animals with visual landmarks show no significant difference in migration success compared to those without.
query_variants: visual landmarks animal migration navigation

objective_decomposition.objective: Investigate the causal mechanisms behind animal migration, including visual landmarks, geographic cues, solar and stellar compass mechanisms, magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms, electromagnetic induction, and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Focus on cross-taxon and cross-environment mechanisms, treating evidence as context-compatible only when appropriate.
objective_decomposition.domain: Research domains: Sensory and Navigation Ecology | Migration Biology | Animal Behavior and Cognition | Ecology Objective: Investigate the causal mechanisms behind animal migration, including visual landmarks, geographic cues, solar and stellar compass mechanisms, magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms, electromagnetic induction, and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Focus on cross-taxon and cross-environment mechanisms, treating evidence as context-compatible only when appropriate.
objective_decomposition.research_brief: Create a new natural-science research project and run the complete AutoGen closed-loop validation workflow: ZhiZhi literature retrieval and PaperGraph   expansion, TanXi primary-mechanism gap detection, Socrates evidence-bounded causal contracts and mediator discovery, MingLi competing falsifiable hypothesis generation with a   structured experimental protocol, YanZhen adversarial counterevidence retrieval and controversy matrix, DuZhi Socratic challenge, and BianLun evidence-weighted minimum-   commitment synthesis. Use semantic_scholar, arxiv, biorxiv, and pubmed where available; retrieve at least 50 results and import at least 20 eligible papers; run debate iteration   with up to 5 rounds. Topic: How do migratory animals know where they are and where they are going? Animal migration is regular seasonal movement among habitats and spans north-   south, east-west, ocean-land, altitudinal, and water-column movement. Treat this as a cross-taxon and cross-environment causal-mechanism question covering birds, insects,   mammals, fish, marine animals, and other organisms only when evidence is context-compatible. Compare visual landmarks and geographic cues; solar and stellar compass mechanisms;   magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms; electromagnetic induction in electrically sensitive aquatic animals; and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Do not   treat evidence from one species, sensory ecology, developmental stage, or migration environment as automatically transferable to another. For every candidate causal edge   preserve taxon, species, model system, migratory stage, environment, perturbation, readout, time scale, supporting sources, challenge sources, alternative pathways, applicable   boundary, and uncertainty state. Prioritize direct mechanism gaps, causal mediation gaps, contradictions, anomalies, and cross-hypothesis coupling over method-scenario-benchmark   coverage holes. YanZhen must actively search for counterevidence and ask whether magnetic mechanisms are necessary, sufficient, context dependent, or bypassed by visual,   celestial, olfactory, inertial, and learned-route cues. If a mediator remains unknown, return MECHANISM_DISCOVERY_REQUIRED with candidate mediators rather than inventing a   complete mechanism. Generate competing and falsifiable hypotheses; experimental protocols must remain execution-blocked unless domain experts explicitly supply model system,   intervention, controls, timepoints, primary and orthogonal readouts, replication, bias control, analysis plan, meaningful effect threshold, interval requirement, power   assumptions, rescue criteria, regime-shift tests, and reproducibility details. At the end, output project_id, PaperGraph paper count, primary scientific gaps, Socrates contract   states, generated hypothesis ids, YanZhen verdict, BianLun final decision, structured experiment protocol status, execution authorization status, remaining uncertainty, and the   next discriminating experiment.

combination_hypothesis.statement: The combination of visual, geographic, solar, magnetic, and electromagnetic mechanisms collectively guide animal migration.
combination_hypothesis.integration_test: A comprehensive study that integrates all mechanisms and tests their combined effect on migration success.

retrieval: Create a new natural-science research project and run the complete AutoGen closed-loop validation workflow: ZhiZhi literature retrieval and PaperGraph expansion, TanXi primary-mechanism gap detection, Socrates evidence-bounded causal contracts and mediator discovery, MingLi competing falsifiable hypothesis generation with a structured experimental protocol, YanZhen adversarial counterevidence retrieval and controversy matrix, DuZhi Socratic challenge, and BianLun evidence-weighted minimum-commitment synthesis. Use semantic_scholar, arxiv, biorxiv, and pubmed where available; retrieve at least 50 results and import at least 20 eligible papers; run debate iteration with up to 5 rounds.
gap_detection: Prioritize direct mechanism gaps, causal mediation gaps, contradictions, anomalies, and cross-hypothesis coupling over method-scenario-benchmark coverage holes.
hypothesis: Generate competing and falsifiable hypotheses; experimental protocols must remain execution-blocked unless domain experts explicitly supply model system, intervention, controls, timepoints, primary and orthogonal readouts, replication, bias control, analysis plan, meaningful effect threshold, interval requirement, power assumptions, rescue criteria, regime-shift tests, and reproducibility details.
verification: YanZhen must actively search for counterevidence and ask whether magnetic mechanisms are necessary, sufficient, context dependent, or bypassed by visual, celestial, olfactory, inertial, and learned-route cues. If a mediator remains unknown, return MECHANISM_DISCOVERY_REQUIRED with candidate mediators rather than inventing a complete mechanism.

decomposition_rules: Each sub-hypothesis must expose an intervention, observable outcome, falsification condition, and evidence window.
A combined conclusion is admissible only after the relevant component hypotheses are independently evaluated.
Missing quantitative bounds remain explicitly unresolved; they must not be fabricated from the objective.

research_question_card.research_question: Visual landmarks in animal migration Presence of visual landmarks -> Landmark recognition and navigation -> Successful migration to destination Animals with visual landmarks show no significant difference in migration success compared to those without.
research_question_card.domain: Research domains: Sensory and Navigation Ecology | Migration Biology | Animal Behavior and Cognition | Ecology Objective: Investigate the causal mechanisms behind animal migration, including visual landmarks, geographic cues, solar and stellar compass mechanisms, magnetic-particle or biogenic-mineral mechanisms, electromagnetic induction, and radical-pair or cryptochrome magnetoreception. Focus on cross-taxon and cross-environment mechanisms, treating evidence as context-compatible only when appropriate.
causal_questions: What is necessary or sufficient for the proposed effect?
What mediates the relationship, and what alternative paths remain plausible?
Under which model, stage, time, or boundary condition does the relationship fail?

boundary_policy.instruction: Use the stated project boundary when assigning a paper role.

paper_role_policy.CORE: Directly supports the bounded research question or supplies context-matched mechanism evidence.
paper_role_policy.BRIDGE: Supplies transferable mechanism, terminology, or background but cannot alone support the core causal claim.
paper_role_policy.EXCLUDE: Conflicts with an explicit project boundary or final domain gate and must not enter core reasoning.
